title: Fluffy Spicy Croatian Rice Pilaf
description: A vibrant, one‑pot rice dish packed with fresh thyme, a splash of deglazed broth, and a gentle kick of spice—perfect for a quick vegetarian dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in garlic, smoked paprika, and red pepper flakes; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Deglaze the pan with a splash of vegetable broth, scraping up any browned bits.
Add the rice, stirring to coat it in the aromatic mixture.
Pour in the remaining vegetable broth and water; bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 12 minutes.
Stir in the frozen peas, fresh thyme, and lemon zest; replace the lid and continue cooking for another 5 minutes until the rice is fluffy and liquid is absorbed.
Remove from heat, fluff the rice with a fork, and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with a drizzle of extra olive oil or a sprinkle of chopped fresh thyme.
